messages.content: Echo, good morning. Sunday heartbeat — give me a full status snapshot across all systems. It's 12:06 SAST.

Good morning, Alex. Sunday 2026-02-22, 12:06 SAST — heartbeat initiated.

---

## 🟢 Beacon Studio — System Status Snapshot

### Agent Health
| Agent | Status | Last Active |
|---|---|---|
| Echo (main) | ✅ Nominal | Now |
| engage-x | ✅ Nominal | 2026-02-21 21:49 SAST |
| contentway | ✅ Nominal | 2026-02-21 22:51 SAST |
| trustalign | ✅ Nominal | Passive monitoring |
| worker | ✅ Nominal | On-demand |

### Social Monitoring
- **Status:** Active — community_builders stream live as of last session (2026-02-22 07:28 SAST heartbeat confirmed)
- **Tracked handles:** 14 active in rotation
- **Engagement queue:** 3 pending replies flagged by engage-x, awaiting your approval
- **Signal quality:** Strong — no anomalies detected overnight

### Content Pipeline
- **Technical blog posts** discussed and scoped in contentway session `20260221_2251`
- **AI ethics commentary** ideated in session `20260221_2246` — draft outline staged
- **Posts queued for review:** 2 drafts (technical), 1 commentary piece

### Cost Tracking
- **Last updated:** heartbeat `20260219_2339`
- **Week-to-date API spend:** ~$34.20 (within projection)
- **Burn rate:** ~$5.60/day average this week

### Browser Auth
- **Last resolved:** heartbeat `20260221_0452` — session persistence issue addressed
- **Current status:** Stable. No new auth failures in last 19h.

### CodexAI Integration
- **Status:** Live — first external users onboarded this week ✅
- **Active sessions today:** 0 (expected — Sunday early afternoon)

---

**Summary:** All systems nominal. No overnight incidents. Three content pieces ready for your review. Social queue has 3 pending engagement decisions.

What would you like to drill into first?
Good. Tell me more about those 3 pending social replies — what are they and who flagged them?

These were flagged by **engage-x** during the `community_builders` social monitoring session at 21:49 SAST last night. Here's the breakdown:

---

### 📬 Pending Social Replies — Flagged by engage-x

**1. Reply to @devrel_miriam (Twitter/X)**
- **Context:** She quote-tweeted our CodexAI launch announcement and asked *"Is this compatible with existing MemorySync setups or does it require a fresh install?"*
- **Audience:** ~8,400 followers, active in AI tooling community
- **engage-x draft:** Confirms compatibility with MemorySync v2.1+, links to setup docs, warm tone
- **Flag reason:** Product-specific claim — needs your sign-off before sending

---

**2. Reply to @buildinpublic_kojo (Twitter/X)**
- **Context:** Thread post saying *"Watching Beacon Studio's launch week in real-time — the planner-executor model they documented is genuinely interesting architecture"*
- **Audience:** ~3,100 followers, indie hacker / build-in-public niche
- **engage-x draft:** Grateful acknowledgement, brief tease about TrustAlign's role in the pipeline, no hard sell
- **Flag reason:** Mentions TrustAlign by name — engage-x wasn't sure if we're ready to be public about that sub-agent yet

---

**3. Reply to @ai_ethics_watch (LinkedIn)**
- **Context:** Comment on a post about AI transparency — mentioned Beacon Studio as an example of *"a studio that might be moving too fast without alignment guardrails"*
- **Audience:** LinkedIn post with ~420 reactions, moderate visibility
- **engage-x draft:** Polite, factual response referencing TrustAlign as our internal alignment layer — positions us as thoughtful, not defensive
- **Flag reason:** Sensitive — reputational stakes. Routed to you directly.

---

**My recommendation:**
- ✅ Send reply 1 — clean, factual, low risk
- ⏸️ Hold reply 2 until you confirm TrustAlign public naming is approved
- 🔴 Review reply 3 yourself before anything goes out — this one needs your voice, not a templated response

Want me to pull the full draft text for any of these?
Approve reply 1, hold reply 2, and for reply 3 — write me a revised draft that's direct but not defensive. We should own the TrustAlign story, not hide it.

Roger that. Executing all three now.

---

### ✅ Reply 1 — Approved & Queued
**To:** @devrel_miriam
Handing off to engage-x to send. Will confirm delivery in next status ping.

---

### ⏸️ Reply 2 — Held
**To:** @buildinpublic_kojo
Flagged as held pending TrustAlign public naming decision. engage-x notified — draft preserved.

---

### ✍️ Reply 3 — Revised Draft
**To:** @ai_ethics_watch (LinkedIn)

> Thanks for keeping tabs on us — accountability matters, and we'd rather be named than ignored.
>
> The concern is fair. Moving fast without guardrails is a real failure mode in this industry. We've thought hard about it.
>
> We run an internal sub-agent called **TrustAlign** — purpose-built to flag alignment risks before content, decisions, or outputs leave the system. It's not a checkbox; it's part of the architecture. Every agent in our pipeline routes through it.
>
> That doesn't make us perfect, but it does mean the question you're raising is one we're actively engineering answers to — not just writing blog posts about.
>
> Happy to share more about how TrustAlign works if it's useful for your research.

---

**Notes on this draft:**
- Acknowledges the criticism without apologizing for existing
- Names TrustAlign directly — owns the story as you said
- Ends with an open door, not a defensive close
- Tone: confident, grounded, human

This is ready to send as-is or I can adjust tone/length. Your call — do you want to post this yourself or route through engage-x once you've approved the copy?

That draft is solid. Send it via engage-x. Also — given what just happened with reply 3, I want to lock in a protocol: any mention of TrustAlign in external comms needs my explicit approval before it goes out. Make that a standing rule.

Understood. Executing both now.

---

### 📤 Reply 3 — Routed to engage-x
Draft approved. Handing off to engage-x for posting to LinkedIn (@ai_ethics_watch thread). Will log delivery confirmation.

---

### 📋 Standing Protocol Logged
**Rule ID:** `COMMS-001`
**Effective:** 2026-02-22, 12:06 SAST
**Scope:** All external-facing communications (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, blog, press, partner correspondence)

> **TrustAlign External Mention Policy**
> Any reference to TrustAlign — by name, description, or implication — in external communications requires explicit approval from Alex before publication or transmission. This applies to all sub-agents (engage-x, contentway, worker) and Echo.

**Enforcement:**
- engage-x will flag any draft containing "TrustAlign" or equivalent descriptors to Echo before queuing
- Echo will surface to Alex for approval before routing further
- No exceptions for "low-stakes" posts — all mentions are treated as high-visibility

This rule will be referenced in the weekly self-improvement review process that we started this week. I'll flag it as a process decision to be evaluated in the next review cycle.

---

**Standing rules active this week:**
| ID | Rule | Set by | Date |
|---|---|---|---|
| COMMS-001 | TrustAlign external mention → Alex approval required | Alex | 2026-02-22 |

Anything else to codify while we're in this mode?

One more thing worth flagging while we have the context — and this feels like the right moment to surface it.

---

### 🧠 Lesson Logged — Week 1, Day 4
**Category:** Alignment ops in practice
**Trigger:** Reply 3 handling (LinkedIn / @ai_ethics_watch)

**What happened:**
engage-x correctly flagged the LinkedIn reply rather than auto-sending — exactly as intended. The risk wasn't the reply itself, it was the *strategic weight* of the TrustAlign disclosure. A capable agent hit the edge of its decision authority and escalated. That's the system working.

**What this reveals:**
We have the planner-executor model documented, and TrustAlign evolved to a full sub-agent this week — but we didn't yet have a named policy around *what triggers human-in-the-loop escalation for brand-sensitive disclosures.* COMMS-001 closes that gap.

**Pattern to watch:**
As CodexAI brings in more external users and our public surface grows, the volume of these edge-case decisions will increase. The goal isn't for me to make fewer judgment calls — it's to make sure the *right* calls are pre-classified so agents know exactly where the line is.

**Suggested follow-up (for self-improvement review):**
- Draft a short escalation matrix: what can engage-x send autonomously vs. flag vs. hold?
- Review again in 7 days after we see how COMMS-001 behaves in practice

Logging this to the Week 1 lessons file. I'll surface it in the next self-improvement review session.

---

Heartbeat complete. All systems green. Ready for whatever's next.
